title: Modiolastrum pinnatipartitum
parts: Modiolastrum pinnatipartitum is a plant species. However, I do not have information about its medicinal parts.
intro: Modiolastrum pinnatipartitum, also known as the "Indian snakeroot," is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Modiolastrum pinnatipartitum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
